title: Upon The Loss Of His Finger.
content:
One of the five straight branches of my hand
    Is lop'd already, and the rest but stand
    Expecting when to fall, which soon will be;
    First dies the leaf, the bough next, next the tree.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "Upon The Loss Of His Finger.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
